items.title: Voice mode is rolling out now in Claude Code. It’s live for ~5% of users today, and will be ramping
items.raw_summary:
Voice mode is rolling out now in Claude Code. It’s live for ~5% of users today, and will be ramping through the coming weeks.

You'll see a note on the welcome screen once you have access. /voice to toggle it on! https://t.co/P7GQ6pEANy
items.why_it_matters: Voice mode in Claude Code represents a fundamental shift in how developers interact with coding agents — speaking code instead of typing it.
items.action: Check if you have access via /voice

items.raw_summary:
Yesterday we reached an agreement with the Department of War for deploying advanced AI systems in classified environments, which we requested they make available to all AI companies.

We think our deployment has more guardrails than any previous agreement for classified AI deploy

items.title: Is Traditional Software Engineering Dead?
items.raw_summary:
Is Traditional Software Engineering Dead?

“Does this mean that traditional software engineering is dead? Absolutely not. Software engineers—even the ones who are not necessarily tuning or training AI models—these are now among the most leveraged people on earth. Sure, the guys who are training and tuning models are even more leveraged because they’re building the tool set that software engineers are using.

But software engineers still have two massive advantages on you. First, they think in co
items.why_it_matters: Naval Ravikant's argument that software engineers are now among the most leveraged people on earth reframes the AI displacement narrative into an amplification story.
items.action: Reflect on how AI tools multiply your engineering leverage

items.title: 🦔 Jason Calacanis says his company hit $300/day per agent using Claude's API at only 10-20% capacit
items.raw_summary:
🦔 Jason Calacanis says his company hit $300/day per agent using Claude's API at only 10-20% capacity, which scales to around $100,000/year per agent. Chamath Palihapitiya added that he's now asking "what's the token budget for our best devs?" and said AI-assisted developers need to be at least 2x as productive just to justify the cost. He said this is actively happening inside his company or he'll run out of money.

My Take
This was always the obvious trajectory. AI providers subsidized usage t
items.why_it_matters: Real production data showing $300/day per Claude agent at only 10-20% capacity reveals the true cost curve of agentic AI and forces ROI calculations on AI-assisted development.
items.action: Calculate your own per-agent token costs and ROI thresholds

items.title: I go offline for one week:
items.raw_summary:
I go offline for one week:
- DoW saga
- everyone from trump to katy perry posts about us
- Claude hits 1 on app store
- sign-up records broken, servers are melting
- oh also we shipped a ton of new stuff in record time

starting to think this is what the takeoff feels like
items.why_it_matters: Alex Albert's summary captures a pivotal week for Anthropic — DoW controversy, App Store #1, server strain, and rapid feature shipping — all happening simultaneously.
items.action: Review Anthropic's recent feature launches you may have missed

items.title: 斯坦福大学竟然开了一门 Vibe Coding 课程，PPT、阅读材料、作业全部公开可用！🫡
items.raw_summary:
斯坦福大学竟然开了一门 Vibe Coding 课程，PPT、阅读材料、作业全部公开可用！🫡

课程编号 CS146S，全名叫《The Modern Software Developer》，持续 10 周，依次讲解了提示词工程、Agent 架构、MCP、上下文工程、安全攻防、Code Review、自动做 App 和上线运维，还请了 Claude Code 创始人、a16z 合伙人来当嘉宾，阵容很豪华。

以我自己的体验来看，这门课更偏工程体系，覆盖了 AI 编程的整个软件开发生命周期，是一套完整的方法论。不过内容比较硬核，不太适合零基础的同学，刚入门的同学可以先跟我的免费开源《Vibe Coding 零基础入门教程》快速实战上手，等能独立做项目了再来啃这门课，效果最好。

从 Karpathy 提出 Vibe Coding 到斯坦福正式开课，前后也就大半年，很多国内高校也开了 Vibe Coding 课程，说明 Vibe Coding 是真的站稳脚跟了，不是昙花一现。

你们学校或公司开始搞 AI 编程培训了吗？
items.why_it_matters: Stanford CS146S formalizing vibe coding into a 10-week curriculum with Claude Code's creator as a guest speaker legitimizes AI-assisted development as a discipline.
items.action: Review the open course materials for structured AI coding practices

items.title: BREAKING: An anonymous dev on GitHub just built an AI that codes and browses the web at the same tim
items.raw_summary:
BREAKING: An anonymous dev on GitHub just built an AI that codes and browses the web at the same time.

It's called Accomplish and it runs locally without burning through API credits.

No Claude Desktop. No Cursor. No monthly subscriptions.

100% Opensource. https://t.co/qoyUVIzcj8
items.why_it_matters: Accomplish — a local, open-source agent that codes and browses simultaneously without API costs — offers a zero-cost alternative for developers priced out of cloud agents.
items.action: Try Accomplish for local agent experimentation
